question: Find the Texas counties appear in both of these groups: (1) counties with at least 10% adult with disability population and at least 23% 65+ elder population in 2023, and (2) Abilene-region counties with 150 or more completed Adult Protective Services investigations in 2023. Of those counties, pick the one with the fewest completed child abuse/neglect investigations in 2023. A leader of that county's Regulators died in that county's seat. What is the county seat of the county where this leader was born? Please return the county seat name only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
practical_motivation: A social services researcher studying the intersection of elderly care and child protection in rural Texas counties wants to identify communities with high rates of vulnerable adult populations that also have significant protective services activity. By comparing child welfare investigation rates between qualifying counties, the researcher can focus on the county with relatively lower child caseload to understand its historical development. Tracing a leader of the Lincoln County Regulators who died in the county seat and then identifying the county seat of his Alabama birthplace provides historical context for migration patterns into the county.

1.fact: In FY2023, 14 Texas counties had at least 10% adult disabled (18-64) population AND at least 23% population aged 65+: Culberson (12.8%, 27.3%), Eastland (10.0%, 24.3%), Edwards (10.8%, 31.8%), Hudspeth (13.2%, 25.8%), Kent (13.6%, 34.1%), King (10.3%, 29.1%), Kinney (12.9%, 24.9%), Lampasas (11.1%, 23.2%), Montague (10.1%, 24.2%), Sabine (12.3%, 31.2%), San Augustine (11.1%, 28.9%), San Jacinto (10.4%, 23.5%), Trinity (12.7%, 28.7%), and Tyler (11.4%, 23.4%).
1.subquestion: Which Texas counties had at least 10% adult disabled population and at least 23% elderly population in FY2023?

2.fact: In FY2023, five Texas counties in Region 2-Abilene had 150 or more completed APS investigations: Brown (232), Eastland (150), Montague (166), Taylor (1207), and Wichita (1446).
2.subquestion: Which Texas counties in Region 2-Abilene had 150 or more completed APS investigations in FY2023?

3.fact: In FY2023, Eastland County had 149 completed child abuse/neglect investigations.
3.subquestion: How many completed child abuse/neglect investigations did Eastland County have in FY2023?

4.fact: In FY2023, Montague County had 193 completed child abuse/neglect investigations (summed across all priorities: 22 + 5 + 166 = 193).
4.subquestion: How many completed child abuse/neglect investigations did Montague County have in FY2023?

5.fact: The Lincoln County Regulators had three leaders: Richard "Dick" Brewer (first), Frank McNab (second), and Doc Scurlock (third and final).
5.subquestion: Who were the leaders of the Lincoln County Regulators?

6.fact: Doc Scurlock died in Eastland, Texas. He was born in Tallapoosa County, Alabama.
6.subquestion: Which of <node_5 answer> died in the county seat of the county with fewer CPI investigations (Eastland County, 149 < 193)?

7.fact: Tallapoosa County's county seat is Dadeville.
7.subquestion: What is the county seat of the Alabama county where <node_6 answer> was born?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: APS populations FY2023, Texas counties with Adult Disabled 18-64 >= 10% AND 65+ >= 23% -> {Culberson, Eastland, Edwards, Hudspeth, Kent, King, Kinney, Lampasas, Montague, Sabine, San Augustine, San Jacinto, Trinity, Tyler}
Node 2: APS investigations FY2023, Region 2-Abilene counties with >= 150 completed investigations -> {Brown, Eastland, Montague, Taylor, Wichita}
Note: other node 1 counties are not in Region 2-Abilene; Brown, Taylor, Wichita do not meet the population criteria
HOP 2 (d=2, data.gov CPI - nodes 3-4 depend on <hop 1 answer>):
Node 3: CPI investigations for Eastland County (from cpi-3-1) -> 149
Node 4: CPI investigations for Montague County (from cpi-3-2) -> 193
Node 5: Lincoln County Regulators Wikipedia -> leaders were Richard "Dick" Brewer, Frank McNab, Doc Scurlock
HOP 4 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 6 depends on <hop 2 answer> and node 5):
Node 6: Doc Scurlock Wikipedia -> Doc Scurlock died in Eastland, Texas (the county seat of Eastland County) and was born in Tallapoosa County, Alabama
HOP 5 (d=1, Wikipedia - node 7 depends on <hop 4 answer>):
Node 7: Tallapoosa County, Alabama Wikipedia -> county seat is Dadeville
